Notice Of Motion And Omiton To Quash Pltfs' Depiosition Notices, Or Alternatively, For Protective Order; Memo Of P&A; Declaration
Off Calendar Mar-21-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
William E. Von Behren, Esq., of Von Behre & Hunter, Tel. 310.607.9111, appearing for Defendant and moving party via Court Call.
Lawrence Mann, Esq., of Bourhis & Mann, Tel. 415.392.4660, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. The court vacated its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT OHIO NATIONAL LIFE ASSURANCE CORPORATION'S Motion To Quash Plaintiff's Person Most Knowledgeable Deposition Notices, Or Alternatively, For Protective Order is denied. The deposition is to occur at the defense counsel's office on a date agreeable to both parties. No sanctions are awarded. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Off Calendar Feb-23-2012 Text Ruling

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S Motion To Dismiss is granted as unopposed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Not reported. =(302/CK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anne Burr, Esq., from Office of the Attorney General, 415-703-1403, for Insurance Commissioner Dave Jones
Christopher S. Milligan, Esq., from Meserve, Mumper & Hughes, LLP, 213-620-0300, for Defendant Ohio National Life Assurance Corporation, appearing by telephone

Discussed and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT DAVE JONES AS COMMISSIONER OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff is granted leave, if he is able to do so in good faith, to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572) A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
